questions.question: Define Boolean algebra and explain the three fundamental Boolean operations with truth tables.
keyPoints: Boolean algebra definition - George Boole, binary values 0 and 1
AND operation - output 1 only when all inputs are 1
OR operation - output 1 when at least one input is 1
questions.modelAnswer: Boolean algebra is a branch of mathematics dealing with binary variables (0 and 1), developed by George Boole. The three fundamental operations are: (1) AND - outputs 1 only when both inputs are 1, denoted as A.B; (2) OR - outputs 1 when at least one input is 1, denoted as A+B; (3) NOT - inverts the input, denoted as A'.

questions.question: Explain XOR and XNOR operations. How are they different from OR and NOR?
keyPoints: XOR - output 1 when inputs are different
XNOR - output 1 when inputs are same
Difference: OR outputs 1 for (1,1) but XOR outputs 0

questions.question: Evaluate the Boolean expression F = A.B' + C.(A+B) for all combinations of A, B, and C.
State and prove the commutative, associative, and distributive laws of Boolean algebra.
keyPoints: Commutative: A.B = B.A and A+B = B+A
Distributive: A.(B+C) = A.B + A.C and A+(B.C) = (A+B).(A+C)

questions.question: What is operator precedence in Boolean algebra? Evaluate: A + B.C' + D.E
keyPoints: Precedence order: Parentheses > NOT > AND > OR
Finally add all terms with OR
